name: Sid Bernstein
abstract: Sidney Bernstein (August 12, 1918 – August 21, 2013) was an American music promoter, talent manager, and author. Bernstein changed the American music scene in the 1960s by bringing the Beatles, the Rolling Stones, Herman's Hermits, the Moody Blues, and the Kinks to America.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: And even in his quartet, when he had Lionel Hampton. I should mention Goodman on clarinet, but Goodman on clarinet, Lionel Hampton on vibes, Teddy Wilson on bass, Sid Bernstein on --

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: Because the promoter was the same promoter that Stan often played dates at Carnegie Hall for and so on—Sid Bernstein.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
